commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5b39af5bc26fa2f0110993a2a79918e69908519f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex native-execution policy checks that block app-server-owned native execution when OpenClaw exec resolves to `host=node`, plus runtime exec-default resolution, focused tests, changelog, and a small docs link fix.

Reproducibility: yes. this is source-reproducible: current `main` checks config/overrides for `host=node` but not the persisted session entry used by the reported `/exec host=node` flow. I did not run a live node setup in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The protected maintainer label plus SDK API and security-boundary tradeoffs require human maintainer review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(codex): honor node exec policy for native surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a Codex native execution policy resolver that blocks app-server-owned execution when OpenClaw exec resolves to `host=node`.
- Apply the policy to direct app-server execution methods, native compaction, `/btw`, native `/codex` command paths, and bound app-server conversation turns.
- Keep normal Codex harness turns usable by preserving OpenClaw `message`, `exec`, and `process` tools, and preserve the first-party CLI node binding path.

Fixes #85012

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/exec-defaults.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/request.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- Direct AWS Crabbox E2E proof: `cbx_64e17300a509`, run `run_491fc4ac98e4`, exit `0`.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server native execution surfaces no longer bypass OpenClaw `exec host=node`; corrected CLI node binding still works.

Real environment tested: Direct AWS Crabbox Linux environment, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_64e17300a509`, run `run_491fc4ac98e4`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --script /private/tmp/openclaw-85012-codex-node-policy-proof.sh`

Evidence after fix: The Crabbox proof exercised direct app-server process execution, MCP reload, native compaction, `/btw`, native `/codex` command paths, CLI node binding, bound app-server turns, normal harness dynamic tools, runtime-policy `sandboxSessionKey`, and explicit `host=auto` override.

Observed result after fix: The proof printed `OPENCLAW_85012_CODEX_NODE_POLICY_E2E_PROOF=passed` and the Crabbox run exited `0`.

What was not tested: A real OpenAI/Codex account-backed model turn was not required for this fix proof because the guarded behavior is resolved before app-server/model execution; the CLI node binding path was exercised through production command handlers with a mocked node-session lookup.
target.author: joshavant

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
